home.name: Svetoslav Hristov
home.title: Software Engineer
home.bio: Curious by nature and structured in practice, I turn complex challenges into clear, reliable systems. I work across software development, cloud-native platforms, DevOps, data and automation.
home.quote: Build with creativity, ship with precision.

about.heroParagraph1: Beyond engineering, I am a positive and easy-going person who enjoys staying active, spending time in nature and reading. In addition, I have entrepreneurial tendencies and enjoy turning ideas into practical projects. At the same time, I dabble in generating AI video content and currently I am building new personal projects I would be glad to discuss further.
about.heroParagraph2: Sports are a big part of my routine as well. I enjoy watching football, basketball, combat sports and some motorsports. I lift weights regularly to stay disciplined, active and focused.

projects.title: EstateVisio
projects.summary: An AI-powered real estate video service that turns property photos into professional marketing videos — complete with cinematic tour, virtual furnishing reveals, and a personalized AI avatar presenter — delivered in under 24 hours.
projects.description: EstateVisio was born from a simple frustration: property presentation shouldn't require coordinating a videographer, scheduling site visits, or waiting days for an edit. The service takes a set of property photos and returns a professionally crafted marketing video — a fast-paced tour montage, layered virtual furnishing reveals that show empty spaces transformed, and optionally an AI-generated presenter that uses the agent's own face and voice. Every video is manually reviewed and montaged for full realism before delivery. The result is a premium showcase that was previously only accessible to high-budget listings, now available for any property — rental, mid-range, or luxury — in under 24 hours.
features: Cinematic property tour montages assembled from photos — no videographer or site visit needed
Layered virtual furnishing reveals — virtual staging applied to video, not just static photos
AI avatar presenter cloned from the agent's own face and voice — personal brand at scale
Manual quality review on every video — 100% realism guaranteed, not automated output
Under 24-hour turnaround from photo delivery to finished video
Free sample video for new prospects — zero-risk way to experience the product
challenges: Building client trust in AI-generated video for professional real estate use
Maintaining 100% realism across diverse property types while keeping turnaround under 24 hours
Creating a done-for-you service workflow that scales without sacrificing quality

projects.title: Polymarket Trading Bot
projects.summary: An AI-powered autonomous trading agent that monitors prediction markets in real time, uses a multi-stage LLM pipeline to triage opportunities, and manages risk across thematic market baskets — all surfaced through a live web dashboard.
projects.description: The bot continuously monitors hundreds of prediction markets, polling for price movements and liquidity signals. When a market shows meaningful activity, it is routed through a two-stage AI pipeline: a lightweight triage model that rapidly classifies the opportunity, followed by a deeper reasoning model that sets strategic direction for the basket it belongs to. Markets are grouped into thematic baskets — each with its own risk budget and steering state — so the system can hold different views on correlated events simultaneously. A real-time dashboard tracks portfolio exposure, AI inference costs, live market prices, and decision history. All analysis and execution is fully asynchronous: data polling, AI reasoning, and risk checks run on independent cycles, keeping latency low without coupling fast signals to slow deliberation.
features: Continuous market monitoring with decoupled polling and analysis cycles — fast data, deliberate decisions
Two-stage AI pipeline: lightweight triage for rapid signal classification, deeper model for strategic steering
Thematic market baskets with independent risk budgets, steering modes, and position limits
Live web dashboard with portfolio equity curve, AI cost tracking, market price charts, and decision log
Persistent state across restarts — cost history, basket assignments, and steering parameters all survive a reboot
Volume-ranked catch-all basket ensures high-liquidity markets are always covered regardless of topic
challenges: The system runs three fully independent async loops: a market data poller at 10-second intervals, a per-market analysis scheduler that gates LLM calls by elapsed time, and a broadcast loop that pushes state to the dashboard every 5 seconds. None of these block each other.

Markets are grouped into thematic baskets — each with its own steering state, risk budget, position limits, and AI confidence level. The top-volume basket automatically absorbs the highest-liquidity unassigned markets, ensuring broad coverage without manual configuration.

The AI layer uses a two-model pipeline: a lightweight triage model runs on every signal, and a deeper reasoning model is only invoked on ESCALATE-classified events or after a minimum review interval. This keeps inference costs proportional to actual market activity.

architectureDiagram.subtitle: Four decoupled stages — data polling, AI analysis, risk management, and live monitoring run on independent async cycles.

internalSteps.label: Triage Model
internalSteps.note: Lightweight fast model classifies each signal as CLEAR, AMBIGUOUS, or ESCALATE.

internalSteps.label: Routing Decision
internalSteps.note: CLEAR signals are skipped cheaply. ESCALATE triggers the deeper reasoning model.

internalSteps.label: Reasoning Model
internalSteps.note: Strategic model sets basket bias, trading mode, and quote width multiplier.

internalSteps.label: Basket Steering
internalSteps.note: Updated steering state persists across markets and survives bot restarts.

projects.title: Tracepack
projects.summary: Multi-household food expiry tracker with a daily email digest and weekly waste summary. Households share a join code, log groceries with expiry dates, and get alerted before items go bad.
projects.description: Tracepack started as a frustration with food waste at home — buying the same spinach twice because nobody tracked the first bag. The app lets multiple people in a household log groceries, see a live urgency dashboard (expires today / this week / fine), and receive a daily digest email listing everything expiring within 2 days. A weekly summary email shows how many items were actually wasted, creating a feedback loop that changes behaviour over time.
features: Multi-household with invite code join flow
Urgency-grouped dashboard (expires soon / this week / fine)
Daily email digest of items expiring within 2 days
Weekly waste summary email (only sent if items were wasted)
Monthly stats chart (added vs consumed vs wasted)
Configurable SMTP — ships with Mailhog for local testing
challenges: Designing the soft-delete + waste stats relationship so deleted items don't silently disappear from historical counts
APScheduler timezone handling — choosing UTC-based cron jobs with a configurable hour to avoid surprises in production
Keeping the email digest useful without being noisy: skipping sends when there are no urgent items

projects.title: lambdaforge
projects.summary: Open-source Python CLI for local AWS Lambda testing. Generates realistic trigger event payloads and invokes handlers locally with moto-based AWS service mocking — no Docker, no SAM.
projects.description: lambdaforge eliminates the friction of local Lambda testing. It generates complete, schema-accurate event payloads for API Gateway, SQS, S3, SNS, and EventBridge triggers, and invokes your handler directly in-process. AWS service calls (DynamoDB, S3, SSM, SQS, SNS) can be intercepted by moto with a single flag. Published on PyPI.
features: Generates schema-accurate event payloads for API Gateway, SQS, S3, SNS, and EventBridge triggers
Invokes Lambda handlers directly in-process — no Docker or SAM required
moto-based AWS service mocking with a single flag (DynamoDB, S3, SSM, SQS, SNS)
Published to PyPI for easy installation via pip
challenges: Designing payload schemas that closely match real AWS trigger shapes across multiple event sources
Integrating moto transparently without requiring changes to the user's handler code
Packaging and publishing a developer tool to PyPI with a clean, minimal CLI interface

projects.title: TikTok Clip Farm — Clipper Module
projects.summary: An automated video processing pipeline that ingests raw TikTok livestream recordings, identifies viral moments with AI, cuts vertical 9:16 clips, burns karaoke-style captions, and delivers finished content to Google Drive — ready for upload.
projects.description: The Clipper Module is the processing core of a three-part automated content pipeline. It downloads raw livestream recordings from Google Drive, transcribes them with GPU-accelerated Whisper, runs a conservative LLM correction pass to preserve dialect and slang, then uses GPT-4o to score 5-minute transcript windows for virality and return precise timestamps. ffmpeg cuts the selected moments into letterboxed 9:16 vertical clips, and an ASS caption engine burns word-by-word karaoke highlights with pause-aware grouping. Finished clips are uploaded back to Drive and picked up by the Upload Module — no shared process, no message queue, just files.
features: GPU-accelerated speech-to-text via faster-whisper (large-v3) with language-aware initial prompt for dialect accuracy
LLM transcript correction pass — conservative by design, preserving slang and non-standard speech rather than normalising it
GPT-4o virality scoring over chunked 5-minute transcript windows, returning precise start/end timestamps for each highlight
ffmpeg clip cutting with letterbox 9:16 padding — TikTok-compliant output without cropping any content
Word-by-word karaoke captions in ASS format, grouped by pause gaps >0.5s to prevent stale words reappearing after silence
Whisper runs in an isolated subprocess, preventing CUDA cleanup crashes from propagating to the parent pipeline
Google Drive as the sole integration layer — finished clips are delivered without coupling the modules to a shared runtime
challenges: Transcription subprocess isolation — Whisper runs in a fully contained child process, keeping CUDA lifecycle entirely separate from the main pipeline so teardown is clean and the parent always exits gracefully.

Two-stage LLM transcript pass — faster-whisper with a language-primed initial prompt captures dialect and slang; GPT-4o then runs a conservative correction pass that improves accuracy without normalising non-standard speech.

Pause-aware karaoke captions — word groups are split on silence gaps >0.5s rather than fixed word counts, so caption boundaries align with natural speech rhythm and the result reads as authored content.
Letterbox 9:16 encoding — ffmpeg pads to TikTok dimensions without cropping, preserving the full frame across any source resolution or aspect ratio with a single resolution-agnostic command.

internalSteps.label: GPT-4o Correction
internalSteps.note: Conservative transcript cleanup — preserves dialect and slang

internalSteps.label: GPT-4o Highlight Detection
internalSteps.note: Chunked transcript windows scored for virality

internalSteps.label: ffmpeg Clip Cutting
internalSteps.note: Letterboxed 9:16 output, fast-seek for VFR livestreams

architectureDiagram.subtitle: Three decoupled modules connected through Google Drive as the storage backbone.

projects.title: CronVault
projects.summary: A self-hosted cron job management dashboard. Schedule jobs, track run history with output logs, and get notified via webhook or email when failures cross a configurable threshold.
projects.description: CronVault grew from the need to reliably schedule and observe recurring tasks without depending on external services. A FastAPI backend drives an APScheduler instance that executes shell commands on a cron schedule and records every run — status, duration, and captured stdout/stderr — to a SQLite database. A Next.js frontend surfaces a live job table, per-job run history with an output log modal, and a duration bar chart. The notifications layer lets operators configure a failure threshold and recovery alerts that fire over HTTP webhook or SMTP email, keeping alert noise low by triggering only on threshold crossings rather than every individual failure.
features: Create, edit, and delete cron jobs with a schedule expression and shell command
Live dashboard table with last-run status, duration, and next scheduled time
Per-job run history with stdout/stderr output in a log modal
Duration bar chart per job for spotting performance regressions
Configurable failure-threshold alerts — fires once at the crossing, not on every failure
Recovery notifications when a job returns to success after hitting the threshold
Dual notification channels: HTTP webhook POST and SMTP email, independently toggleable
Dockerised setup — backend, frontend, and scheduler in one compose stack
challenges: Designing the scheduler so job edits take effect immediately without restarting the process
Keeping notifications low-noise: firing at the threshold crossing only and resetting cleanly on recovery
Capturing subprocess stdout/stderr reliably while enforcing a per-run timeout
